goal: Reconcile I4 docs and skill worker into session sources workflow

summary.title: Reconcile I4 docs and skill worker
summary.what: Merged the verified I4 docs/skill worker commit into the canonical session-sources workflow branch and updated workflow-state, session-registry, and wave-map to mark Wave 3 complete and E1 ready.
summary.why: The orchestrator control plane must remain the durable source of truth before launching E1, so I4 proof, commit, intent, and next-wave state were recorded in canonical docs.
summary.user_goal: Reconcile I4 docs and skill worker into session sources workflow

decisions.chose: Mark I4 verified and closed with commit 22b0d95 and intent int_be172a7e.
decisions.rationale: The worker completed handoff, commit, Mainline seal, lint, CLI smoke, and npm run check.

decisions.chose: Move workflow state to Wave 4 E1 ready.
decisions.rationale: I1-I4 implementation and docs slices are reconciled, so the next dependent step is evidence verification.

decisions.chose: Record I4 Mainline conflicts as low-confidence same-workflow overlap rather than blockers.
decisions.rationale: The reported overlaps were shared README/skill/workflow files from the current stacked workflow, not contradictory source behavior.

review_notes: Actual canonical reconciliation files are docs/workflows/2026-06-05-session-sources/session-registry.md, workflow-state.md, and wave-map.md.
The seal prepare file list is wider because the branch is stacked on the full session-sources workflow; this intent only claims the final I4 control-plane update.
Verification: git diff --check passed; npm run check passed with 27 files and 152 tests.

architectural_claims: I1-I4 implementation and docs slices are reconciled before E1 starts.
wave-map.md is the context-compaction recovery point for the remaining E1/R1 waves.
behavioral_changes: No runtime behavior changed in this control-plane commit.
